WeiQin Chuah

Lecturer (ECR) · RMIT University, Melbourne

School of Mechanical, Mechatronics and Manufacturing Engineering.
Member of the Intelligent Automation Research Group.
Ph.D. RMIT University, 2022.

Research Interests

Robust Representation Learning

  • Large-scale self-supervised learning
  • Foundation models for vision
  • Shortcut avoidance & OOD robustness
  • Test-time adaptation

Geometric Computer Vision

  • 3D scene understanding
  • Neural rendering (NeRF & 3DGS)
  • Depth estimation & stereo matching
  • AR / VR

AI for Engineering

  • Anomaly detection & quality inspection
  • Digital twin modelling
  • Robotics
  • Mapping & localisation

Publications

2026

Modernizing Livestock Operations: Smart Feedlot Technologies and Their Impact
S.D. Dao, A.K. Gostar, R. Tennakoon, W.Q. Chuah, A. Bab-Hadiashar
Animals, 16(8), 1244, 2026
Joint Modeling of Corruption-Driven and Information-Limited Uncertainty for Robust 3D Gaussian Splatting
Z. Hui, A.K. Gostar, W.Q. Chuah, A. Bab-Hadiashar, R. Tennakoon
IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV), 2026
Logit-Adjusted Test-Time Adaptation under Partial Class Imbalance
T. Weerasinghe, R. Tennakoon, W.Q. Chuah, A. Bab-Hadiashar
IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV), 2026

2025

MANU2167 Design for Assembly and Automation

Prospective Students

I am actively recruiting PhD and Master by Research students
  • Geometric computer vision
  • Robust representation learning for vision
  • AI for engineering

If you are interested in working with me, please get in touch with your CV and a brief research statement.
